cards.title: Startup Battlefield 200 Applications Close June 8
cards.body: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this October in San Francisco.

cards.title: Google Pays SpaceX $920M Monthly for Compute
cards.body: Google and SpaceX finalized a deal worth $920M per month for compute resources. The agreement stems from unexpected demand for Google's recently launched AI products.

cards.title: AirTrunk Commits $30B for India AI Data Centers
cards.body: Australian data center operator AirTrunk announced plans to build 5GW of AI data center capacity in India. The massive investment reflects the growing infrastructure demands of AI companies.

cards.title: AI Industry Faces Runaway Cost Management Crisis
cards.body: Industry conversation has shifted from aggressive scaling to implementing guardrails and cost controls. Companies are scrambling to manage AI's rapidly escalating operational expenses.

cards.title: Anthropic Reports $47B Annualized Revenue Before IPO
cards.body: Anthropic's annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, dramatically up from $9 billion at end of 2025. The company is growing at breakneck pace ahead of its IPO filing.

cards.title: Airbnb CEO Plans New AI Lab Launch
cards.body: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky announced plans to launch a new AI lab. Previously, the company opted against LLM partnerships, stating existing products weren't ready.

cards.title: Meta Builds AI Data Centers in Tents
cards.body: Meta discovered a creative cost-cutting solution: constructing data centers using tents. The approach aims to slash the company's massive data center infrastructure costs.

cards.title: Poke Approved as First AI Agent on iMessage Business
cards.body: Poke became the first AI agent approved for Apple's Messages for Business platform. The startup enables users to interact with AI agents through simple text messages.

cards.title: Meta Launches AI Creator Assistant on Facebook
cards.body: Meta released a new AI assistant for Facebook creators that answers questions about posting times and comment sentiment. The tool eliminates the need to manually parse performance dashboards.

cards.title: Hello Robot Releases Fourth-Generation Stretch Home Robot
cards.body: California startup Hello Robot unveiled Stretch, the fourth generation of its home assistance robot. The robot is designed to help bring robotics into residential settings.

cards.title: Lovable Expands Google Cloud Deal 5x with Claude Access
cards.body: Lovable signed a multiyear expansion deal with Google Cloud involving a 5x increase in usage footprint. The deal includes expanded access to Anthropic's Claude AI model.

cards.title: Alphabet Raises Record $85B for Google AI Business
cards.body: Alphabet completed a record-breaking $85 billion stock sale dedicated to Google's AI initiatives. The massive raise signals strong investor confidence in AI-related business opportunities.

cards.title: Google Dreambeans Turns Your Life Into Cartoon
cards.body: Google launched Dreambeans, an AI tool that converts personal data from your Google account into illustrated stories. The whimsically-named tool creates AI-generated cartoon narratives.

cards.title: Amazon Shows AI-Generated Product Images in Search
cards.body: Amazon will now display AI-generated product images matching user search queries using visual search technology. The retailer says the feature helps guide customers to relevant products.

cards.title: Apple App Store Hits $1.4T in Annual Billings
cards.body: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion the previous year. Digital goods sales reached $149 billion with 90% of transactions paid without Apple commission.

cards.title: Google pays SpaceX $920M monthly for AI compute
cards.body: Google signed a major deal with SpaceX to secure computing capacity for its AI products. The agreement reflects unexpected demand surge for Google's recently launched AI offerings.

cards.title: AI infrastructure costs spiral out of control
cards.body: The industry is shifting from rapid expansion to managing runaway AI costs. Companies are implementing guardrails and control measures as the 'tokenmaxxing' era ends.

cards.title: Anthropic's revenue soars to $47B annualized
cards.body: Anthropic announced explosive growth with annualized revenue reaching $47 billion in May, up from $9 billion at end of 2025. The company faces testing ground ahead of its IPO.

cards.title: Apple approves first AI agent for Messages
cards.body: Poke became the first AI agent approved for Apple's Messages for Business platform. The startup enables users to deploy AI agents through simple text messages.

cards.title: Meta launches AI assistant for Facebook creators
cards.body: Meta rolled out an AI creator assistant that answers performance questions without parsing dashboards. Creators can now ask when to post and what audiences are saying.

cards.title: Lovable expands Google Cloud deal by 5x
cards.body: Lovable signed a multiyear deal with Google Cloud to expand its footprint significantly. The agreement includes expanded access to Anthropic's Claude model.

cards.title: Alphabet raises record $85B for Google AI
cards.body: Alphabet completed a record-breaking $85 billion stock sale dedicated to Google's AI business. The move signals strong investor confidence in AI-related ventures.

cards.title: Google's Dreambeans turns your life into cartoons
cards.body: Google introduced Dreambeans, an AI tool that curates illustrated stories from your personal data. The quirky offering transforms real moments into animated narratives.

cards.title: Amazon deploys AI-generated product images
cards.body: Amazon is using visual search and AI to show generated product images in search results. The retailer claims AI imagery helps guide users to relevant products.

cards.title: Meta's AI agent hacked, Instagram accounts stolen
cards.body: Attackers exploited Meta's AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts by redirecting them to attacker-controlled emails. The breach exposed gaps in AI security beyond typical safeguards.

cards.title: AI chatbots reshape how our brains work
cards.body: Psychologists warn AI chatbots may be affecting cognitive control and attention spans. Research shows significant impacts on how users interact with digital technology.

cards.title: Courts overwhelmed by AI-generated lawsuits
cards.body: Federal judges report a flood of AI-generated legal filings, many with errors and weak cases. Courts are struggling to cope with volume as pro se litigants use AI tools.

cards.title: Small businesses leverage AI for operations
cards.body: Small businesses can deploy AI across accounting, design, and market research functions. LLMs now enable entrepreneurs to automate skills that previously required specialized hires.

cards.title: Agentic AI transforms global healthcare
cards.body: AI agents are being deployed to address healthcare sector strain and chronic underinvestment. Autonomous systems can help fill gaps in fragmented care access.

cards.title: These LLMs best resist Russian propaganda
cards.body: Certain large language models demonstrated superior resistance to Russian propaganda compared to others. The findings highlight important differences in model robustness.
